basics.description: Deploy Azure Policy to automatically apply diagnostic settings to your Azure resources, forwarding logs to a Log Analytics workspace.

basics.name: workspaceName
basics.label: Log Analytics Workspace Name
basics.toolTip: The name of the existing Log Analytics workspace that diagnostic logs will be sent to. Must be 4-63 characters.

constraints.validationMessage: Only alphanumeric characters and hyphens are allowed, and the value must be 4-63 characters long.

options.text: Select which resource types you want to automatically apply diagnostic settings to. Azure Policy assignments will be created at subscription scope, meaning any new resources of the selected types will also have diagnostic settings applied automatically. A remediation task will also run to catch any existing non-compliant resources. Requires Owner on the subscription.

elements.name: enableDiagnosticPolicies
elements.label: Select resource types to enable diagnostic settings for
elements.toolTip: Diagnostic settings will be configured on all matching resources found in your subscription at deployment time.

allowedValues.label: Azure Key Vault
allowedValues.description: Enables AuditEvent diagnostic logs on all Key Vaults in the subscription, forwarding to the Log Analytics workspace.

allowedValues.label: Azure Network Security Groups
allowedValues.description: Enables NetworkSecurityGroupEvent and NetworkSecurityGroupRuleCounter logs on all NSGs in the subscription.

allowedValues.label: Azure Storage Accounts
allowedValues.description: Enables StorageRead, StorageWrite and StorageDelete logs on blob, queue, table and file services for all Storage Accounts in the subscription.

allowedValues.label: Azure SQL Databases
allowedValues.description: Enables SQLSecurityAuditEvents, SQLInsights, Errors, Timeouts, Blocks and Deadlocks logs on all SQL Databases in the subscription.

options.text: Optionally exclude specific Storage Accounts from having diagnostic settings applied. Enter each account as resourceGroupName/storageAccountName, separated by commas. For example: rg-prod/mystorageaccount1,rg-prod/mystorageaccount2. Both the resource group name and storage account name are visible directly in the Storage Accounts list view in the Azure Portal without needing to open the resource.

elements.name: excludeStorageResources
elements.label: Storage Accounts to exclude (comma separated)
elements.toolTip: Enter each Storage Account to exclude as resourceGroupName/storageAccountName, separated by commas. Example: rg-prod/mystorageaccount1,rg-prod/mystorageaccount2
